title: 💻 Best Practices for Claude Code
content:
Claude Code 技术干货

Unlike a chatbot that answers questions and waits, Claude Code can read your files, run commands, make changes, and autonomously work through problems while you watch, redirect, or step away entirely. Plan Mode is useful, but also adds overhead.For tasks where the scope is clear and the fix is small (like fixing a typo, adding a log line, or renaming a variable) ask Claude to do it directly.Planning is most useful when you’re uncertain about the approach, when the change modifies multiple files, or when you’re unfamiliar with the code being modified. For a full overview of extension features and when to use each one, see [Extend Claude Code](/docs/en/features-overview). During long sessions, Claude’s context window can fill with irrelevant conversation, file contents, and commands. * [Claude Code desktop app](/docs/en/desktop#work-in-parallel-with-sessions): Manage multiple local sessions visually. * [How Claude Code works](/docs/en/how-claude-code-works): the agentic loop, tools, and context management. * [Extend Claude Code](/docs/en/features-overview): skills, hooks, MCP, subagents, and plugins.
excerpt: Unlike a chatbot that answers questions and waits, Claude Code can read your files, run commands, make changes, and autonomously work through problems...

title: 💻 Claude Code: [PDF] The Complete Guide to Building Skills for Claude | Anthropic
content:
Claude Code 技术干货

Before you start Identified 2-3 concrete use cases Tools identified (built-in or MCP) Reviewed this guide and example skills Planned folder structure During development Folder named in kebab-case SKILL.md file exists (exact spelling) YAML frontmatter has --- delimiters name field: kebab-case, no spaces, no capitals description includes WHAT and WHEN No XML tags (< >) anywhere Instructions are clear and actionable Error handling included Examples provided References clearly linked Before upload Tested triggering on obvious tasks Tested triggering on paraphrased requests Verified doesn't trigger on unrelated topics Functional tests pass Tool integration works (if applicable) Compressed as .zip file After upload Test in real conversations Monitor for under/over-triggering Collect user feedback Iterate on description and instructions Update version in metadata 30 Reference B: YAML frontmatter Required fields ---name: skill-name-in-kebab-case description: What it does and when to use it.
excerpt: Before you start Identified 2-3 concrete use cases Tools identified (built-in or MCP) Reviewed this guide and example skills Planned folder structure ...

title: 💻 Claude Code Updates: Introducing /simplify and /batch Skills ...
content:
Claude Code 技术干货

Claude Code Updates: Introducing /simplify and /batch Skills, Enhanced UX, and More | daily.dev. Claude Code Updates: Introducing /simplify and /batch Skills, Enhanced UX, and More. Claude Code (New Updates): New SIMPLIFY & BATCH Skill Agents, Better UX, New Commands & MORE! Claude Code received several notable updates including two new skill agents: /simplify and /batch. The /simplify command uses parallel agents to improve code quality, tune performance, and enforce claude.md compliance after changes are made. The /batch command enables large-scale code migrations by running dozens of isolated agents in parallel using git worktrees, each handling its own files and submitting PRs. Additional updates include a new /copy command to copy the last Claude response to clipboard, markdown rendering in the ask-user-question tool for visual option previews, and HTTP hook handlers that allow integrating Claude Code with external services for validation, logging, or CI pipeline triggers. Claude Code is getting two new Skills in its next version: /simplify and /batch.
excerpt: Claude Code Updates: Introducing /simplify and /batch Skills, Enhanced UX, and More | daily.dev. Claude Code Updates: Introducing /simplify and /batch...

title: 💻 Claude Code Batch Processing Complete Guide - SmartScope
content:
Claude Code 技术干货

#!/bin/bash# parallel_processing.sh # Implement parallel processing()  { local  file = " $1 "  local  output_dir = " $2 "  echo  "Processing: $file "    "Optimize this code for performance"   " $file "   " $output_dir/$( " $file ") "} # Execute in parallel export      "*.py"  |    4   {}  echo  "✅ Parallel processing completed". #!/bin/bash# robust_processing.sh set    # Stop on error # Log configuration LOG_FILE ="claude_batch.log" exec  1  (  " $LOG_FILE ") exec  2  (  " $LOG_FILE "  & 2) # Error handling function()  { local  line_number = " $1 "  echo  "❌ Error occurred at line: $line_number "  echo  "📝 Check log file: $LOG_FILE "  exit  1} trap  'handle_error $LINENO'  # Process execution()  { local  max_retries = 3  local  retry_count = 0  while  ;  do  if    "Process these files"   300     ;  then  echo  "✅ Processing completed successfully"  return  0  else  (())  echo  "⚠️ Attempt $retry_count failed, retrying..."   5  fi  done  echo  "❌ Processing failed after $max_retries  attempts"  return  1} # Main processing()  { echo  "🚀 Starting batch processing..."  # Pre-processing  if  ;  then  echo  "❌ Input file not found"  exit  1  fi  # Execute processing   # Post-processing  if  ;  then  echo  "✅ Batch processing completed successfully"  else  echo  "❌ Output file is empty or missing"  exit  1  fi}  " $@ ".
